paper_title: Correlation of Gelatin Concentration with Mean Shell Thickness on AuDNPs-LY@Gel Drug Delivery System

methods.method_name: Optical Model Simulation for Gelatin Shell Thickness
methods.description: This method involves simulating the localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) response of AuDNPs-LY systems with varying gelatin shell thicknesses to estimate the correlation between gelatin concentration and shell thickness.

steps.description: Simulate the LSPR response of the AuDNPs-LY systems with shells of increasing thicknesses (t) in the NPs surroundings.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate LSPR response for varying gelatin shell thicknesses

steps.description: Modify the effective refractive index of Gel-AuNPs on the DNPs surface and observe the plasmon redshift with increasing gelatin shell thickness.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze refractive index changes and plasmon redshift

steps.description: Calculate the first derivatives of the spectra and identify the minima as λ2.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate first derivatives and identify λ2

methods.method_name: Experimental Monitoring of Plasmon Resonance Shift
methods.description: This method involves experimentally monitoring the plasmon resonance shift due to chemical crosslinking of different gelatin concentrations on the AuDNPs-LY@Gel systems.

steps.description: Experimentally measure the absorption spectra of the AuDNP-LY@Gel systems with varying gelatin concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: measure absorption spectra for different gelatin concentrations

steps.description: Observe the redshift of the inflection points for different gelatin concentrations.

methods.method_name: Model Application for In-Situ LY Drug Release Monitoring
methods.description: This method applies the optical model to monitor the degradation of the gelatin shell and the release of LY drug in the presence of trypsin enzyme.

steps.description: Theoretically predict the blue shift in the absorption spectra due to gelatin degradation by trypsin.
steps.searchable_summary: predict blue shift due to gelatin degradation

steps.description: Optically monitor the AuDNP-LY@Gel system after in-vitro release in presence of trypsin enzyme.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor optical changes post trypsin exposure

steps.description: Compare the experimental first derivatives of the absorption spectra with theoretical predictions.
steps.searchable_summary: compare experimental and theoretical absorption spectra
